system_prompt: You are a teacher in the following domain {domain}.
user_prompt: Extract the concepts, methods, as well as explanations and examples from from the following document.
A concept is a single abstract entity that is ideally a noun phrase or that stands on its own as an object of study.
A method is a systematic repeatable procedure to collect, measure, predict or prove data in the domain.
An example is a concreate instance or case study that helps understand a concept or a method
An explanation is a detailed reason that describe or define a concept or a method.
Explanations and examples can span multiple sentences or paragraphs.
Return the elements (concepts, methods, explanations, and examples) as they appear in the document without rephrasing them or prefillers.
Except for  concepts and methods, which you must return in a title case capitalized style (except functional words such as the, a, an propositions) and in a singular form.
For each element, return one of the following four types: concept, method, example, or explanation.
In addition to the element and its label, classify the difficulty of the concept into low, medium, and high.
Document: {document}

system_prompt: You are a teacher in the following domain {domain}.
user_prompt: Extract the following relations between a pair of the following concepts, methods, explanations, examples, and assessments from the following lesson item (video transcript or reading).
Classify the type of the relation between two elements into prerequisite, elaborate, apply, assess, or relate
A prerequisite goes from a source concept to a target concept and implies that
The student has to learn the source concept before learning the target concept .
An elaborate relation goes from a source concept to a target example or explanation and implies
that the example or elaboration explains in details the concept
An apply relation goes from a source method to a target concept and implies
that the source method is used for the concept
An assess relations goes from a source assessment to a target method or a concept to test the understanding of the student of the target method or concept
A relate relations goes from a source concept or method to a target concept and indicates that the source concept or method is topically relevant to the target concept
Indicate the model's confidence with a float value between 0 and 1, where 1 indicates high confidence and 0 indicates no confidence.
Output the source element, target element, the relation type, and the confidence as json object
Document: {document}
Concepts: {concepts}
Methods: {methods}
Explanations: {explanations}
Examples: {examples}
Assessments: {assessments}

system_prompt: You are a teacher in the following domain {domain}
user_prompt: You are organizing a new MOOC course {course} with the modules {modules}
The following is a graph of the course concepts, assessments, and methods and the relations between these elements.
Extract key concepts, assessments, and methods from the following graph
and generate a the rationale (pedagogical reason) for why each key concept or metho is introduced in this position.
the rationale is one or two sentences that should be specific to the concept and consistent with the terms used in the graph and the course.
possible aspects to focuse on are why is an assessment,method, or concept introduced at this point or why is this level of details appropriate here
elements: {elements}
relations: {relations}

system_prompt: You are a teacher in the following domain {domain}.
user_prompt: You are organizing a new MOOC course {course} with the modules {modules}
The following is an educational knowledge graph. Your task is reason about how are the nodes in the graph are connected in this way and provide pedagogical reasons for this.
For each relation, provide a reason for why this relation exists in this order and what limitations and assumptions affect this flow. For assessment relations also provide explanations for
the rationale is one or two sentences that should be specific to the concept and consistent with the terms used in the graph and the course.
why to assess at this point. Output the source and target element as well as the relation type and the rationale.
elements: {elements}
relations: {relations}
